data.title: Consumer sentiment revised down in August, even as inflation expectations ease in
data.description:
August U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index: 58.2 vs. prior reading of 58.6 and 61.7 in July, according to a University of Michigan survey released on Friday.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +4.8% vs. initial reading of +4.9% in August and +4.5% in July.

Implied five-year inflation: +3.5% vs. +3.9% initial print and +3.4% in July.

Consumer expectations index: 55.9 in August vs. first reading of 57.2 and 57.7 in July.

Current conditions index: 61.7 vs. initial print of 60.9 and 68.0 in prior month.

data.title: Frontier meets with Spirit Air over Spirit's rebuilding efforts - report
data.description:
Spirit Aviation (NYSE:FLYY) has met with Frontier Group (NASDAQ:ULCC) over the airline's rebuilding efforts less than six months after returning from bankruptcy.

Spirit (NYSE:FLYY) Chairman Robert Milton met with Frontier (NASDAQ:ULCC) Chairman Bill Franke earlier this week, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited a person familiar with the matter. 

The talks focused on Spirit's (NYSE:FLYY) rebuilding efforts and the state of the US airline industry, according to the report. There was no talk about an acquisition of Spirit (FLYY). 

Spirit didn’t respond to requests for comment from Bloomberg.

Spirit (FLYY) warned earlier this month that without a cash infusion, it won’t be able to comply with its debt obligations, and is unlikely to survive for another year. 

The talks between Spirit (FLYY) and Frontier (NASDAQ:ULCC) come after Spirit rejected a takeover offer from Frontier in January, following its November bankruptcy filing. 

Shares of Spirit rose 3.2% on Friday.

data.title: Franklin Resources announces launch of Franklin Dividend Growth ETF
data.description:
Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) announced on Friday the launch of Franklin Dividend Growth ETF (FRIZ) that aims to invest in U.S. firms with the potential to generate consistent dividend hikes.

The fund invests in companies across the market-cap spectrum and in a diverse range of industries and sectors.

"FRIZ expands our product suite into the large blend category and gives clients another way to access our dividend expertise in a highly diversified portfolio," said David Mann, head of ETF product and capital markets at Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Chicago PMI drops more than expected in August
data.description:
August Chicago PMI: 41.5 vs. 45.3 consensus and 47.1 prior, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Friday.

A reading under 50 indicates a contraction in the Chicago area manufacturing sector.

data.title: Verizon Communications files mixed securities shelf
data.description: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) has filed a registration statement with the SEC for the proposed sale of mixed securities. The company may offer at one or more times common stock, preferred stock, and debt securities.

data.title: Elastic slips even as as analysts praise Q1 results, guidance
data.description:
Elastic (NYSE:ESTC) shares erased premarket gains and fell slightly in early trading on Friday, even as the enterprise software company reported stronger-than-expected results and guidance, leading to praise from Wall Street.

“Elastic reported impressive 1Q26 results with accelerating revenue growth landing ahead of expectations and provided a better than expected guide, sending shares higher after hours,” DA Davison analyst Lucky Schreiner wrote in a note to clients. “Demand for Elastic's AI Search functionality is increasing as AI and Agentic applications are created and security consolidation trends stood out in the quarter. Price increases did contribute to the outperformance, regardless, sales execution continues to improve as Elastic grows with larger enterprise customers.”

Several other investment firms also praised Elastic's results, including Guggenheim and Stifel Nicolaus, both of which raised their price targets on the stock. Guggenheim moved its price target to $122 from $107, while Stifel upped its target to $134 from $112.

data.title: Autodesk soars after 'strong' Q2 keeps analysts largely bullish
data.description:
Shares of Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) climbed about 12% on Friday after second quarter fiscal 2026 results and outlook surpassed estimates which also saw largely positive reactions from analysts.

Oppenheimer reiterated its Outperform rating and raised the price target on the stock to $375 from $350 on higher growth/margin expectations.

Analysts led by Ken Wong said that Autodesk reported better than expected fiscal second quarter results and increased full-year guidance against lingering macro uncertainty. Fiscal second quarter "core" revenue growth held at 11%, positively tilting the growth bias towards double-digit.

The company's management increased billings by a point (ex. foreign exchange and transition) after second quarter collections came in well ahead of estimates, said the analysts adding that optimistically, management continues to embed pandemic-level conservatism into the low-end of the billings range.

"Autodesk also positively surprised investors with a sneak peek at long-term margin expectations (41%/45% extransition). We remain positive on medium and long-term margins, and now see a clearer path back to double-digit "core" revenue growth," said Wong and his team.

Goldman Sachs reaffirmed its Neutral rating but raised the price target on Autodesk's stock to $320 from $300.

Analysts led by Kash Rangan said they acknowledge several positives from the quarter  Autodesk’s continued momentum from the new Transaction Model; improving new business momentum from channel partners on the back of go-to-market, or GTM, optimization phased in at the start of the year; and AI prioritization and adoption momentum, with Autodesk’s AI modeldelivering more than 1.2M dimensions via AutoConstrain and Fusion.

However, the analysts noted that while they take accept Autodesk’s initial fiscal 2029 operating margin guide of 41% and see potential for the company to execute on this goal, they prefer to wait for further clarity around other elements of the long-term strategic framework (upcoming Investor Day on Oct. 7).

The analysts are also looking for proof points that Autodesk can sustainably accelerate core revenue growth above high-single digits and solidify a healthier growth trajectory.

"Specifically, we see the following factors as key to the path for reacceleration: ACC and Fusion 360, AI product velocity and monetization, and increased cross-sell and upsell momentum post Transaction Model implementation," said Rangan and his team.

Meanwhile, Mizuho reiterated its Outperform rating and raise the price target on Autodesk's stock to $375 from $350.

Analysts at  Mizuho said Autodesk reported strong fiscal second results ($39M revenue beat is among the largest in recent years) driven by accelerating AECO (architecture, engineering, construction, and operations) growth (benefiting from data centers, infrastructure and industrial offset by weakness in commercial) and easing friction from the new transaction model.

"Given the smooth roll-out of the new transaction model and improving competitive positioning, we feel confident that Autodesk can accelerate its growth and profitability in the longer run," said the analysts.

Related stocks: PTC (PTC) was largely flat premarket on Friday.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: MRVL, ADSK, DELL, BABA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures retreated slightly on Friday as investors weighed the release of the Fed’s preferred inflation metric, the PCE report, which came in line with expectations.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Ambarella (NASDAQ:AMBA) +16% - Shares surged after the semiconductor firm reported Q2 revenue growth of 49.9% Y/Y, with results and guidance beating estimates by a wide margin. For Q3, Ambarella expects revenue of $100M–$108M (vs. $91.2M consensus) and adjusted gross margins of 60%–61.5%. Full-year revenue is now projected to grow 31%–35% to $373.2M–$384.6M, well above the $349.8M estimate.
  • Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) +16% - Shares soared after the company's gross merchandise volume logged strong gains in FQ4, helping to deliver an earnings and revenue beat. With that momentum, it issued solid guidance for FY2026 and FQ1. The buy now, pay later lending platform expects FY2026 gross merchandise volume of more than $46B (Visible Alpha consensus $45.7B). Its outlook for full-year revenue is ~8.4% of GMV, or ~$3.86B ($3.89B consensus). Adjusted operating margin is seen at more than 26.1% vs. 24.1% in FY 2025. Affirm (AFRM) expects Q1 revenue of $855M-$885M vs. $858.4M consensus and GMV of $10.1B-$10.4B vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $9.83B. Adjusted operating margin is expected to be 23%-25% vs. 27.0% in Q4 2025.
  • Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) +12% – Shares climbed after the company reported strong Q2 results and issued guidance well above expectations. Revenue rose 17% Y/Y, while remaining performance obligations jumped 24%. For Q3, Autodesk projects adjusted EPS of $2.48–$2.51, above the $2.40 consensus, and revenue of $1.80B–$1.81B vs. $1.77B expected.
  • Elastic (NYSE:ESTC) +9% - Shares jumped after the technology company reported FQ1 results and full-year guidance that topped estimates by a wide margin.CEO Ash Kulkarni highlighted ongoing demand for its differentiated technology and the role of AI in driving adoption of Elastic’s Search AI Platform. For Q2, Elastic guided adjusted EPS of $0.56–$0.58 (vs. $0.59 est.) and revenue of $415M–$417M (vs. $410.2M est.). For FY2026, it projects revenue of $1.679B–$1.689B (vs. $1.66B est.) and adjusted EPS of $2.29–$2.35 (vs. $2.28 est.).
  • Alibaba (BABA) +7% - Shares gained after the Chinese e-commerce giant posted strong revenue growth driven by China’s AI boom, offsetting a surprise profit decline. Customer management revenue climbed 10%, while its Cloud Intelligence Group rose 26%, with AI-related product sales delivering triple-digit growth for the eighth straight quarter. 

Biggest stock losers

  • Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) -15% – Shares tumbled after Q2 results merely matched estimates, despite revenue surging 58% Y/Y, fueled by a 69% jump in data center sales to $1.49B. However, its Q3 outlook disappointed, with projected net revenue of $2.06B falling short of the $2.11B consensus, while adjusted EPS guidance of $0.74 was only slightly above expectations of $0.73.
  • Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) -7% – Shares slipped despite the IT giant delivering Q2 results and guidance that mostly topped expectations. For Q3, Dell projects adjusted EPS of $2.45 (midpoint) on revenue of $26.5B–$27.5B, compared with a consensus of $2.55 EPS on $26.4B in sales. Full-year adjusted EPS is forecast at $9.55 (midpoint), ahead of the $9.40 estimate, with revenue of $105B–$109B vs. $105.03B expected. COO Jeff Clarke highlighted “exceptional” AI demand, with the company now targeting $20B in AI server revenue for fiscal 2026, up from prior guidance.

data.title: Wall Street falls as investors assess in-line PCE report; Marvell also weighs
data.description:
The stock market opened in the red as the July PCE report came in-line, while Marvell's results weighed on Nasdaq. 

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.65%.

July core PCE (excludes food and energy): +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% and +0.3% prior, and +2.9% Y/Y vs. +2.9% consensus and +2.8% prior.

PCE price index: +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.3%  prior, and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.6% prior.

The U.S. international goods trade deficit expanded more than expected.

Wholesale inventories rose 0.2% M/M in July, as expected, and retail inventories rose 0.2%.

Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares plunged 17% in early trade after its recent quarterly results and guidance were nothing short of a disappointment to Wall Street, as investors expressed concern over the company's future in the artificial intelligence spending boom.

Wall Street’s major averages finished higher on Thursday, as investors weighed a wave of economic data and Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest earnings, all as the S&P 500 surpassed 6,500 and marked yet another record close.

"Markets put in another decent performance yesterday, with the S&P 500 at a new record as positive data reassured investors about the near-term outlook. But even as equities hit new highs, investors remain heavily focused on the Federal Reserve’s independence, and today there’s going to be an emergency hearing about Lisa Cook’s position on the Board of Governors that’s due to start at 10am EST," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen. 

In more rate cut news, Fed Governor Christopher Waller said Thursday he favors beginning U.S. interest rate cuts next month and anticipates additional cuts over the next three to six months, aiming to “ease monetary policy and move it to a more neutral stance.”

On the economic side, the August Chicago PMI will be released during market open. The Chicago PMI is seen ebbing to 45.2 in the month versus 47.1 in July.

data.title: TXNM Energy shareholders approve Blackstone acquisition
data.description:
TXNM Energy (NYSE:TXNM) said shareholders voted overwhelmingly to approve Blackstone Infrastructure's $11.5B acquisition of the company at a special shareholders meeting held Thursday.

Under the terms of the proposed agreement, TXNM Energy (NYSE:TXNM) shareholders will receive $61.25 in cash for each share of TXNM Energy common stock held at closing.

The deal still requires regulatory approvals from the New Mexico Public Regulation Commission, Public Utility Commission of Texas, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, U.S. Department of Justice, Nuclear Regulatory Commission and Federal Communications Commission.

Subject to the approvals, TXNM Energy (TXNM) continues to anticipate that the deal closing will occur in H2 2026.
